title: Error Boundary
body:
## Definition

An **error boundary** is a React component that catches JavaScript errors in its **child render tree** and shows fallback UI instead of crashing the whole app.

## Why it exists

Isolate failures (broken widget, bad API shape) so the rest of the app stays usable.

## How it works

Class: `getDerivedStateFromError` + `componentDidCatch`. Libraries: `react-error-boundary`.

Does **not** catch: event handler errors, async code, SSR errors alone, errors in the boundary itself.

## Common confusion

Not a replacement for try/catch in handlers — use both. Log to Sentry in `onError`.

## In practice

Wrap route segments, risky third-party widgets, and data-heavy panels.
bodyLinked:
## Definition

An **[error boundary](concept:error-boundary)** is a React component that catches JavaScript errors in its **child render tree** and shows fallback UI instead of crashing the whole app.

## Why it exists

Isolate failures (broken widget, bad API shape) so the rest of the app stays usable.

## How it works

[Class](concept:es6-class): `getDerivedStateFromError` + `componentDidCatch`. Libraries: [react-error-boundary](concept:error-boundary).

Does **not** [catch](concept:try-catch-finally): event handler errors, [async](concept:async-await) code, [SSR](concept:server-side-rendering) errors alone, errors in the boundary itself.

## Common confusion

Not a replacement for [try](concept:try-catch-finally)/catch in handlers — use both. Log to Sentry in `onError`.

## In practice

Wrap route segments, risky third-party widgets, and data-heavy panels.
